author: ladidan
content:
<@219015859182632960> asks:

> How are EIP-8025 optional proofs expected to work? I see https://github.com/eth-act/lighthouse optional-proofs and https://github.com/eth-act/zkboost. The latter points at an EL and an ERE server.
> 
> Is the idea that there's a public --proof-engine-endpoint? Or does the current design envision running additional services locally, in addition to a CL and EL?

<@466102668138512394> <@402080271563751435> for visbility

author: taulepton_
content:
A participant who wants to generate proofs (opt in) will run an EL and a  "proving cluster", which is orchestrated by zkboost. 

A participant that exclusively wants to verify proofs will run a zkboost sidecar in verify mode, and the CL will invoke zkboost via the proof node API to verify proofs. However, this may change in the future as we are exploring embedding the verification keys and logic within the CL itself.

zkboost is a local service - there is no public endpoint. This is critical because a client does not want to "outsource" proof verification to a third party, as this would introduce a trust assumption that the external party is verifying the proofs correctly.

There is potential for a public endpoint to request proof generation from - however, this is not planned currently.
